statement: Nothing outside the engine mutates a mem-repo; all writes route through MCP, UniFFI, or the CLI.
Reads may use any surface, but writes may not.
The write boundary is enforced by discipline, not by capability — the files are editable, but raw edits are disallowed.
A raw write would bypass two guarantees: schema validation at write time, and the provenance record of why the write happened.
Every update is guarded by an optimistic content-hash check: the caller presents the expected hash of the current state.
If the on-disk state moved since the caller read it, the hashes disagree and the write is refused rather than applied.
The losing writer re-reads the new state and retries; no write is ever silently lost.
A mem-repo is shared at once by a long-lived engine process, out-of-band CLI invocations, a human, and several agents.
The coherence rule is reload-before-operation.
A polling file-watcher detects when the mem-repo ref has moved.
A mem_changed notice marks the in-memory store stale, and the store reloads from the branch tip before serving the next operation.
A failed writer check-and-set can leave the in-memory store ahead of committed disk, so reads must reconcile against the branch.
The runtime validator gates every ingress; a schema-violating write is refused rather than coerced.
Violations include an unknown entity type, a required field left unset, or a disallowed relationship type.
A refusal carries a typed recovery payload: the declared field list, the allowed values, and a nearest-match suggestion.
Every mutation is a git commit.
Commits carry auto-emitted Tool/Actor/Client trailers.
The require_notes workspace policy nudges each mutating commit to carry a one-sentence prose rationale.
A direct count over the live mem-repo put rationale-note adherence near 97% of mutating commits.
The decision history is readable with git log alone — no engine, no database.
Markdown plus git is the only authoritative store; every index is a derived projection that can be rebuilt from the markdown.
To move a project's knowledge base to another machine you copy the .git directory; the indexes rebuild on first load, with no export step or vendor format.
